THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF FREEMAN, JAMES C. , AN INDIVIDUAL ARCHIBALD, JAMES K. , AN INDIVIDUAL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT HEADLANDS MORTGAGE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION SEE SCANNED DOC - THE DEED OF TRUST SECURING THE LOAN FROM HEADLANDS MORTGAGE COMPANY TO PLAINTIFFS, RECORDED SEPTEMBER 20, 1996 AT THE SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY RECORD'S OFFICE WITH MORTGAGE DOCUMENT NO. G050467, SHOULD HAVE BEEN RECONVEYED TO PLAINTIFFS AFTER THEY FULLY REPAID THE LOAN ON OR ABOUT OCTOBER 24, 1997. THAT DEED OF TRUST IS NOW HEREBY RECONVEYED. ADDITIONALLY, PLAINTIFFS' TITLE TO THE SUBJECT PREMISES IS ESTABLISHED AND QUIETED AS TO ANY AND ALL CLAIMS BY PERSONS OR ENTITIES OTHER THAN LIENHOLDER WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Continued To May-06-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quiet Title; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendants Headlands Mortgage Company, a California corporation.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Geoffrey Murry, Esq. and Hannah Stitt, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs James C. Freeman and James K. Archibald, who were not present for this morning's proceedings.
No appearance by defendant Headlands Mortgage Company, a California corporation.
Plaintiff's Complaint to Quiet Title and for Declaratory Relief was filed on June 7, 2019 and personally served on the authorized agent of process for defendants Headlands Mortgage Company, a California corporation, Wells Fargo & Company, a Delaware corporation, and Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., a National Banking Association, on June 12, 2019.
Notice of Pendency of Action was filed on June 14, 2019.
Default as to defendant Headlands Mortgage Company, a California corporation, was entered on July 22, 2019.
Dismissal as to defendant Wells Fargo & Company, a Delaware corporation, was filed on August 6, 2019.
DOE defendants were dismissed on February 5, 2020.
Dismissal as to defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A., a National Banking Association, was filed on February 6, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiffs James C. Freeman, an individual, and James K. Archibald, an individual, and against the defaulted defendant Headlands Mortgage Company, a California corporation, as follows:

The Deed of Trust securing the loan from Headlands Mortgage Company to plaintiffs, recorded September 20, 1996 at the San Francisco County Record's Office with Mortgage Document No. G050467, should have been reconveyed to plaintiffs after they fully repaid the loan on or about October 24, 1997. That Deed of Trust is now hereby reconveyed.

Additionally, plaintiffs' title to the subject premises is established and quieted as to any and all claims by persons or entities other than lienholder Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Judgment and submit same to the Court for review and signature.

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quiet Title; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Feb-06-2020 Continued To Feb-18-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Motion For Judgment Of Quiet Title; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Geoffrey Murry In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF JAMES FREEMAN, JAMES ARCHIBALD Motion For Judgment Of Quiet Title is OFF CALENDAR for Department 501. Motion for entry of judgment upon entry of default shall be calendared in Department 514. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
